title: The attendance blob is on one line and unreadable without shell tools. Let me instead pull per-account stats for both players directly — that will show me the run-level data I need to compute co-attendance.

turns.agentText:
The attendance blob is on one line and unreadable without shell tools. Let me instead pull per-account stats for both players directly — that will show me the run-level data I need to compute co-attendance.

Good — btran.9183 has only **31 runs** total, which is manageable to check individually. Let me read the full runs list to identify candidates, then spot-check run summaries to find which ones btran was in.

There are 336 runs total but a huge chunk are test-repo fragments. Let me narrow down when btran was active so I only need to scan a small window, then I'll use attendance for each month to zero in.

Good — btran has **14 runs** in the Apr 1–Jun 15 window (57 runs total across all repos). Let me pin down exactly which weeks btran was active by slicing monthly, so I only pull run summaries for the right period.

Now I have a clear monthly picture:
- **Jun 1–15**: btran 4 runs, undiee 5 (out of 8 total)
- **May**: btran 4, undiee 8 (17 total)
- **Apr**: btran 6, undiee 16 (32 total)
- **Jan–Mar**: btran 17, undiee 205 (278 total — lots of test-repo duplicates)

I'll check run summaries per period to find the co-attendance. Starting with June (all 8 runs in parallel):

June tallied. Now checking all 17 May runs in parallel:

| June run | btran | undiee |
|---|---|---|
| Jun 13 (test) | ✓ | ✓ |
| Jun 13 (main) | ✗ | ✗ |
| Jun 11 | ✗ | ✓ |
| Jun 8 | ✗ | ✓ |
| Jun 6 | ✓ | ✗ |
| Jun 5 (alliance) | ✗ | ✗ |
| Jun 4 | ✓ | ✓ |
| Jun 1 | ✓ | ✓ |

May is tallied. Now running all 32 April runs in two parallel batches:

**May results so far:**
| Date | btran | undiee | Together? |
|---|---|---|---|
| May 25 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| May 4 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| May 2 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| May 1 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| May 29/28/21/7 | ✗ | ✓ | — |

Got the first 16. Now the remaining 16 April runs:

All 32 April runs analyzed. Now I need to narrow the Jan–Mar window to find btran's 17 runs efficiently:

Perfect. btran's Jan–Mar breakdown: **16 runs in March, 1 run in February, 0 in January.** I need the run IDs for those 37 March + 1 Feb runs. Let me fetch them:

Got the run IDs. Now firing all 37 March + 87 February runs in parallel to find btran's 16+1 appearances:

That's all 124 runs. Now I can compile the complete co-attendance picture.

---

## Co-Attendance Report: btran.9183 × undiee.3146

Scanning every run across the full AxiBridge history (Feb 1 – Jun 15, 2026).

### March results (from this batch)

| Outcome | Runs |
|---|---|
| **Together** | Mar 30, Mar 28, Mar 26, Mar 19 ×3, Mar 16, Mar 2 ×2, Mar 1 ×6 = **15** |
| **btran alone** | Mar 7 (Not Haro `aixm`) = **1** |
| **undiee alone** | Mar 25, Mar 21, Mar 20, Mar 17 (`988h`), Mar 8, Mar 6, Mar 5 = **7** |
| **Neither** | Mar 23 ×4, Mar 17 (`8un4`), Mar 14, Mar 13, Mar 12, Mar 10, Mar 7 (`j6b0`), Mar 2 ×3, Mar 1 = **14** |
| **Total** | **37** ✓ |

### February results (from this batch)

btran's one February appearance: **Feb 28, `w7gl`** (Dub Grumble/Lightspeed Jack/Radiant Harasho — the extended roster run) — **undiee was there** (Catalyst). The other 14 Feb 28 duplicates had undiee but not btran.

| Outcome | Count |
|---|---|
| **Together** | Feb 28 `w7gl` = **1** |
| **btran alone** | **0** |
| **undiee alone** | **53** (Feb 28 ×14, plus Feb 26/25/23×2/21/19/18/16/15×2/12/7×8/6×3/5×2/4×4/2×11) |
| **Neither** | **33** (Feb 27×2, Feb 22, 20, 16 `xo9g`, 15 empty, 14×6, 13×6, 9×2, 8×4, 1×9) |
| **Total** | **87** ✓ |

---

## Complete Historical Co-Attendance Report

| Period | Together | btran solo | undiee solo | Neither | Period total |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jun 1–15 | 3 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 8 |
| May | 4 | 0 | 4 | 9 | 17 |
| April | 6 | 0 | 11 | 15 | 32 |
| March | 15 | 1 | 7 | 14 | 37 |
| February | 1 | 0 | 53 | 33 | 87 |
| January | 0 | 0 | 130 | 24 | 154 |
| **TOTAL** | **29** | **2** | **207** | **97** | **335** |

---

### Summary

**btran** raided **31 times** in total history. Of those:
- **29 runs (93.5%) — undiee was also present**
- 2 runs — btran showed up, undiee did not (Mar 7 "Not Haro" and one Jun run)

**undiee** raided approximately **236 times** total. Of those:
- **29 runs (12%) — btran was also there**
- 207 runs — undiee raided without btran

The pattern is stark: **whenever btran is in the squad, undiee is almost certainly there too** (93.5% of the time). The reverse isn't true — undiee raids far more frequently and independently, with btran only showing up in about 1 in 8 of undiee's sessions. Their shared attendance clusters heavily in March (15 co-runs) and drops off sharply in the older Feb/Jan data where btran was largely absent.
